message:
v0.3.11: Widen idle noise threshold and bound rate_danger_ratio denominator

Version bump 0.3.10 → 0.3.11.

guardrails.rs:
- Increase idle noise threshold from 1 byte/sec to 10 bytes/sec.
  Production data from trj showed predicted≈-2, actual≈0 during idle
  periods, producing infinite error from noise-level disagreement.
  The wider threshold absorbs minor EWMA lag during idle without
  masking real calibration issues.

- Replace infinity-producing denominator logic with bounded formula:
  denominator = max(|actual|, |predicted|, 1.0). This prevents
  infinity when actual ≈ 0 and predicted is negative (EWMA recovery
  lag after burst), while preserving meaningful ratios when both
  rates are significant. The old code returned INFINITY for any
  actual < 1e-9 with non-zero predicted, which is wrong when the
  underestimation check already filtered safe overestimation.

- Add test case for idle-vs-recovery mismatch (predicted=-500,
  actual=0) verifying bounded ratio ≤ 1.0.
- Add test case for production noise scenario (predicted=-2,
  actual=0) verifying trivial threshold returns 0.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -1,6 +1,6 @@
 [package]
 name = "storage_ballast_helper"
-version = "0.3.10"
+version = "0.3.11"
 edition = "2024"
 license = "MIT"
 description = "System service that prevents disk-full scenarios from coding agent swarms via ballast files, artifact scanning, and special location monitoring."
